title: Mobile-First CSS: Lessons from Building Campus Connect
excerpt: Practical tips for building responsive interfaces that feel native on every screen size.
author: Apoorv Patel
content:
Building Campus Connect taught me that mobile-first isn't just a buzzword—it's a discipline that saves hours of refactoring.

Start with the smallest viewport. Design your base styles for 320px width, then use min-width media queries to enhance for larger screens. This inversion prevents the common trap of designing desktop-first and then awkwardly cramming content on mobile.

Use CSS custom properties for your spacing and typography scales. An 8-point grid system keeps everything aligned: --space-1: 8px, --space-2: 16px, and so on. Consistency breeds professionalism.

Touch targets matter. Apple's HIG recommends 44px minimum. I audit every interactive element against this standard.

Finally, test on real devices. Browser DevTools are helpful, but nothing replaces holding your phone and tapping through the experience.
